title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, retired from the NFL after 13 seasons, announcing his decision on Instagram.
Gilmore played for six teams, winning Super Bowl LIII and earning NFL Defensive Player of the Year in 2019 with the Patriots.
He recorded 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions throughout his NFL career.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle begins global layoffs, potentially impacting up to 30,000 employees, but specific effects on Massachusetts remain unknown.
Oracle's layoff notices began Tuesday, with the company citing organizational changes but not confirming exact totals or reasons.
Massachusetts state filings show no Oracle layoffs as of March 27, despite the company's presence in Burlington and Nashua.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year contract with the Cincinnati Bengals, making them his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger's playing time dropped last season with the Patriots, leading to a trade to the Steelers for a sixth-round pick.
Dugger recorded 42 tackles, two interceptions, and one sack in nine games with the Steelers last season.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes' truck was stolen from Boston Logan Airport's central parking garage after a family vacation.
The theft resembles another recent incident at the same garage; both trucks ended up in New Jersey.
Massachusetts State Police logged the truck as stolen; investigation is ongoing with no arrests made.

title: This registered nurse is running Boston for Special Olympics Massachusetts
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gabby Peduto, a 26-year-old nurse from Wakefield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Special Olympics Massachusetts.
Peduto works with students with complex medical needs and is inspired by their resilience and capabilities daily.
She aims to support Special Olympics Massachusetts' mission of inclusion and empowerment for individuals with disabilities.

title: North Andover police investigate ‘unauthorized’ social media comment about Kelsey Fitzsimmons case
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover Police investigate an 'unauthorized' Facebook comment related to Kelsey Fitzsimmons' civil litigation against the town.
The comment was allegedly posted by a former employee who resigned in 2023 amid allegations of inappropriate conduct.
The police department is reviewing social media access and policies, and plans to restore its channels soon.

title: The Federal closes in Waltham after 7 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Federal, a seafood and steak restaurant at 74 Tower Rd. in Waltham, has closed after seven years.
Rising costs and economic conditions were cited as reasons for the closure, despite hopes for increased local business.
Sister restaurant Stazione di Federal will remain open and honor all outstanding gift cards from The Federal.

title: Your spring reading list, mapped
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com highlights 16 new books perfect for spring, covering a range of moods and reading speeds.
The spring reading list is organized into a matrix, offering fast-paced, slow-burning, light, and dark options.
Readers can sign up to receive the full spring book matrix and top recommendations directly via email.

title: American Express is opening a lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: American Express will open a two-story Centurion Lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport's Terminal C in 2029.
The 20,000 sq. ft lounge includes premium amenities, influenced by Boston's historic and coastal themes.
Massport approved a $75 million budget, while AmEx committed $391 million for the lounge's development.

title: ‘Thank you for 8 great years!’: Fenway Johnnie’s has closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Fenway Johnnie's at 96 Brookline Ave. has closed after 8 years near Fenway Park.
The sports bar's closure surprised customers, many considering it a pre-event staple for Red Sox games and concerts.
West End Johnnie’s at 138 Portland St. remains open despite the Fenway location's closing.
